---
title: "About"
---
*WEBSITE STILL WIP*


Hi there! I’m Edward, an undergrad in the class of 2024 and an Egleston Scholar at Columbia Engineering.

As of now, my main interests lie amidst the fields of Machine Learning and Computational Neuroscience. My lifetime goal is to contribute to the ongoing pursuit of comprehending what exactly governs consciousness and sentience, which will *hopefully* happen some day (and also *hopefully* not create something along the lines of Ultron or Skynet).

Currently, most of my time is going into a research project with Dr. Nakul Verma at Columbia's Computer Science department and a subcontract software engineering job at DATAFLUCT,Inc.

My research is in the field of Representation Learning—specifically, regarding the mathematical properties of the Skip-Gram model. While there's been a significant amount of research on the underlying properties and characteristics of the Skip-Gram model, a lot is still not understood too well and we don't have good explanations as to why the model has its surprising qualities such as additive compositionality and et cetera. That being said, I'm currently trying to see how certain changes in the Skip-Gram model affect downstream tasks.

At DATAFLUCT,Inc, I'm currently working on developing a dialogue system that provides BI (Business Intelligence) and data analytics results as the lead software engineer. Apparently I can't disclose much, but the funny part is almost no Machine Learning is involved so far and most of the logic is pure heuristics. Nevertheless, it's an eye-opening experience working with (way) more experienced developers who are working on different parts of the system.

Besides research and work, I'm also in Columbia Space Initiative (CSI), Columbia's space-themed club. I'm in the RASC-AL team that participates in an annual student competition to design deep space mission concepts. Specifically, I'm in charge of designing the propulsion systems (more or less just reading past papers on the topic and coming up with new designs based on previously successful missions).

While I was in Columbia's Formula Racing Club (FSAE), I was working on tire simulation; specifically, applying curve-fitting and coefficient optimization on Pacejka's Magic Formula.

In my free time, I like to program—mainly simulations that I find interesting to see what would happen and reinforcement learning agents to train on the games I make. I also like making robots and tinkering (and Columbia has the perfect space for that—the Makerspace).

If you're interested, here are a few miscellaneous links (most are in Japanese) where I appear:

- [The Egleston Scholars: Weida (Edward) Li](https://www.engineering.columbia.edu/egleston-scholars/weida-edward-li)
- [江副記念財団学術部門第四十九期生：李 為達](https://www.recruit-foundation.org/academic/li/)
- [Containerインタビュー：李 為達](https://container-web.jp/people/15564.html)
- [現役高校生チーム、アメリカのロボット大会に臨む！](https://newswitch.jp/p/10882)

Feel free to reach out to me for anything!

- Email: [wl2787@columbia.edu](mailto:wl2787@columbia.edu)
- GitHub: [NarutatsuRi](https://github.com/narutatsuri)
